english: Table for two, please.
usageNote: Use when arriving at a restaurant to request seating.

english: May I see the menu, please?
usageNote: Polite way to ask for the menu.

english: What do you recommend?
usageNote: Ask the server for their top picks.

english: I would like to order this one.
usageNote: Say this while pointing at a menu item.

english: We are ready to order.
usageNote: Signal the server you are ready.

english: I am allergic to peanuts.
usageNote: Critical allergy warning; peanut oil is common in Chinese cooking.

english: I am allergic to seafood.
usageNote: Important in coastal cities where seafood is widely used.

english: I am allergic to dairy products.
usageNote: Useful since some dishes use milk or butter.

english: I am allergic to eggs.
usageNote: Eggs are common in stir-fries and soups.

english: Does this dish contain nuts?
pinyin: Zhège cài lǐ yǒu jiānguǒ ma?
usageNote: Show this before eating if you have nut allergies.

english: I am vegetarian.
usageNote: Short and clear; widely understood in China.

english: I do not eat meat.
usageNote: Clarify no meat at all if needed.

english: I do not eat pork.
usageNote: Pork is very common in Chinese cuisine.

english: I do not eat beef.
usageNote: Some people avoid beef for religious reasons.

english: Less spicy, please.
usageNote: Chinese dishes can be very spicy; adjust to your taste.

english: No MSG, please.
usageNote: MSG is common; some people are sensitive to it.

english: May I have a glass of water, please?
pinyin: Qǐng gěi wǒ yī bēi shuǐ.
usageNote: Tap water is not drinkable in China; you will get boiled or bottled water.

english: No ice, please.
usageNote: Many cold drinks come with ice by default.

english: Can I have the bill, please?
usageNote: 买单 is the standard word for asking for the check.

english: Can I pay with WeChat Pay?
usageNote: WeChat Pay is the most popular payment method in China.

english: Can I pay with Alipay?
usageNote: Alipay is another widely accepted mobile payment.

english: Can I pay by credit card?
usageNote: Not all restaurants accept credit cards; check first.

english: How much is this?
usageNote: Useful when prices are not listed on the menu.

english: This is delicious!
usageNote: A great compliment to the chef or server.

english: Could you pack this to go?
usageNote: 打包 means take-away packing; very common request.

english: Where is the restroom?
usageNote: 洗手间 is the polite term for restroom.

english: Can we have more napkins, please?
pinyin: Néng zài gěi diǎn zhǐjīn ma?
usageNote: 纸巾 means napkins or tissues.

english: I have a reservation.
usageNote: 预订 means reservation; useful for popular restaurants.

english: What is in this dish?
usageNote: Useful for checking ingredients before ordering.

english: Thank you, the food was wonderful.
usageNote: A warm farewell to the staff after your meal.

english: I would like to order the house specialty, please.
pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng diǎn zhèlǐ de zhāopái cài.
usageNote: Use this when you want to try the restaurant's signature or recommended dish.

english: Could you describe this dish? Is it very spicy?
chinese: 能描述一下这道菜吗？辣吗？
pinyin: Néng miáoshù yīxià zhè dào cài ma? Là ma?
usageNote: Useful for understanding unfamiliar menu items or checking the spice level.
audioText: 能描述一下这道菜吗？辣吗？

english: We would like a quieter table, if possible.
chinese: 如果可能，我们想要一个安静点的桌子。
pinyin: Rúguǒ kěnéng, wǒmen xiǎng yào yīgè ānjìng diǎn de zhuōzi.
usageNote: A polite way to request seating in a less noisy area of the restaurant.
audioText: 如果可能，我们想要一个安静点的桌子。

english: Is this dish made with any nuts or peanut oil?
chinese: 这道菜里有坚果或花生油吗？
pinyin: Zhè dào cài lǐ yǒu jiānguǒ huò huāshēng yóu ma?
usageNote: A crucial question for people with nut allergies. Use with caution, as cross-contamination is possible.
audioText: 这道菜里有坚果或花生油吗？

english: I am following a low-sodium diet. Can the chef prepare this without added salt?
chinese: 我遵循低盐饮食。厨师能做这道菜不加盐吗？
pinyin: Wǒ zūnxún dī yán yǐnshí. Chúshī néng zuò zhè dào cài bù jiā yán ma?
usageNote: A specific dietary request. Be clear about your needs to the server.
audioText: 我遵循低盐饮食。厨师能做这道菜不加盐吗？

english: Could we get separate checks, please?
chinese: 请给我们分开结账，好吗？
pinyin: Qǐng gěi wǒmen fēnkāi jiézhàng, hǎo ma?
usageNote: Use this when dining in a group and each person wants to pay for their own meal.
audioText: 请给我们分开结账，好吗？

english: Is the ice in the drinks made from filtered water?
chinese: 饮料里的冰是用过滤水做的吗？
pinyin: Yǐnliào lǐ de bīng shì yòng guòlǜ shuǐ zuò de ma?
usageNote: A practical question in some regions where tap water is not potable.
audioText: 饮料里的冰是用过滤水做的吗？

english: We would like to share this dish. Could you bring an extra plate?
chinese: 我们想分享这道菜。能多拿一个盘子吗？
pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎng fēnxiǎng zhè dào cài. Néng duō ná yīgè pánzi ma?
usageNote: A common request in many Asian dining cultures where dishes are shared family-style.
audioText: 我们想分享这道菜。能多拿一个盘子吗？

english: Can I have this dish without MSG?
pinyin: Zhè dào cài néng bù fàng wèijīng ma?
usageNote: A specific ingredient request for those sensitive to monosodium glutamate.

english: We are in a bit of a hurry. How long will our order take?
chinese: 我们有点赶时间。我们的菜大概需要多久？
pinyin: Wǒmen yǒudiǎn gǎn shíjiān. Wǒmen de cài dàgài xūyào duōjiǔ?
usageNote: Politely inquire about wait times if you have a tight schedule.
audioText: 我们有点赶时间。我们的菜大概需要多久？

english: Could you recommend a local wine or beer that pairs well with this?
chinese: 能推荐一种和这道菜很配的本地酒或啤酒吗？
pinyin: Néng tuījiàn yī zhǒng hé zhè dào cài hěn pèi de běndì jiǔ huò píjiǔ ma?
usageNote: A good way to experience local beverages and enhance your meal.
audioText: 能推荐一种和这道菜很配的本地酒或啤酒吗？

english: Is there a vegetarian version of this dish available?
pinyin: Zhè dào cài yǒu sùshí bǎnběn ma?
usageNote: Asks if a menu item can be prepared without meat or animal products.

english: We'd like to pack the leftovers to go, please.
chinese: 请帮我们把剩下的打包。
pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒmen bǎ shèng xià de dǎbāo.
usageNote: A common request to take uneaten food home. Less common in some high-end restaurants.
audioText: 请帮我们把剩下的打包。

english: Does this contain any shellfish or shellfish extract?
chinese: 这道菜有贝类或贝类提取物吗？
pinyin: Zhè dào cài yǒu bèilèi huò bèilèi tíqǔwù ma?
usageNote: Important for those with shellfish allergies. Ask clearly about hidden ingredients in sauces.
audioText: 这道菜有贝类或贝类提取物吗？

english: Could we have some hot water for tea, instead of cold water?
chinese: 能给我们一些热开水泡茶吗，不要冷水？
pinyin: Néng gěi wǒmen yīxiē rè kāishuǐ pào chá ma, bùyào lěng shuǐ?
usageNote: In many places, especially China, hot water is readily available and often preferred for drinking or tea.
audioText: 能给我们一些热开水泡茶吗，不要冷水？

english: What is this condiment? How is it typically used?
chinese: 这是什么调味料？一般怎么用？
pinyin: Zhè shì shénme tiáowèiliào? Yībān zěnme yòng?
usageNote: Use when you're unfamiliar with a sauce or condiment on the table. Shows curiosity and helps you enjoy your meal.
audioText: 这是什么调味料？一般怎么用？

english: I'm on a gluten-free diet. Does this sauce contain wheat or soy sauce?
chinese: 我吃无麸质饮食。这个酱汁里有小麦或酱油吗？
pinyin: Wǒ chī wú fūzhì yǐnshí. Zhège jiàngzhī lǐ yǒu xiǎomài huò jiàngyóu ma?
usageNote: A specific dietary question. Clarify that soy sauce often contains wheat.
audioText: 我吃无麸质饮食。这个酱汁里有小麦或酱油吗？

english: Could you take a group photo for us, please?
chinese: 麻烦帮我们拍张合影，好吗？
pinyin: Máfán bāng wǒmen pāi zhāng héyǐng, hǎo ma?
usageNote: A polite request to a waiter or staff member. Often well-received.
audioText: 麻烦帮我们拍张合影，好吗？

english: Is the tipping policy included in the service charge?
chinese: 小费是包含在服务费里吗？
pinyin: Xiǎofèi shì bāohán zài fúwù fèi lǐ ma?
usageNote: Important to ask in regions where tipping customs vary. Avoids confusion when paying.
audioText: 小费是包含在服务费里吗？

english: Thank you for the wonderful meal and service.
chinese: 非常感谢，饭菜和服务都很棒。
pinyin: Fēicháng gǎnxiè, fàncài hé fúwù dōu hěn bàng.
usageNote: A sincere compliment to the staff upon leaving. A great way to end your dining experience.
audioText: 非常感谢，饭菜和服务都很棒。
